title: 5.4.2 Prim Algorithm
subtitle: Greedy tree expansion for minimum spanning tree

content.introduction: Prim builds an MST by repeatedly adding the minimum-weight edge that expands the current tree to a new vertex.

sections.title: 5.4.2.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define the growing-tree invariant and edge-cut selection rule in Prim's greedy strategy.
examples: Explain why Prim never forms cycles in growth process
Define key[v] as best crossing edge weight
Contrast Prim growth with Kruskal edge-sorting

sections.title: 5.4.2.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe priority-queue or array-key implementation: initialize start vertex, update candidate edge keys, and expand tree.
examples: Run Prim from vertex A on a weighted graph
Update key and parent after each selection
Output final MST edge set and total weight

sections.title: 5.4.2.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(E log V) with heap and adjacency list, or O(V^2) with matrix; discuss graph-density suitability.
examples: Prefer matrix Prim on dense small graphs
Use Prim for incremental network expansion scenarios

keyTakeaways: Implement key updates and vertex selection
Guarantee MST correctness with cut property
Analyze complexity by data structure choice
Apply Prim effectively on weighted undirected graphs
